src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response package

Submodules

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.arp_cache_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.arp_cache_parser.ArpCacheParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse ARP cache output into structured records.

This parser is locale-agnostic: it does not depend on English labels such as dynamic, static, etc. It only validates fields based on detectable patterns (IPv4, MAC, “incomplete” entries, etc.).

Output format:

-Each ARP entry becomes a JSON object. -The final result is returned as a JSONL string (one JSON per line). -Each record has the fixed type “arp_entry”.

Supported input format:

Typical arp -a or arp -a -v output, where entries follow an interface header such as:

Interface: 192.168.1.10 — 0x12

192.168.1.1 11-22-33-44-55-66 dynamic 192.168.1.42 incomplete

The parser extracts:

-Interface IP -Interface index (hex, string form) -IP address -MAC address (or “incomplete”) -Entry type (raw string if present)

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse ARP cache text into JSONL.

Parameters:

text – Raw textual output from an ARP command (e.g. arp -a).

Returns:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object describing a single entry. Example object:

{

“type”: “arp_entry”, “interface_ip”: “192.168.1.10”, “interface_index”: “0x12”, “ip_address”: “192.168.1.1”, “mac_address”: “11-22-33-44-55-66”, “entry_type”: “dynamic”

}

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.bits_jobs_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.bits_jobs_parser.BitsJobsParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse bitsadmin output into structured JSONL job records.

This parser:

  • Detects job boundaries using GUID: lines.

  • Handles multi-key lines (e.g. TYPE: ... STATE: ... OWNER: ...).

  • Extracts peercaching flag blocks.

  • Parses JOB FILES indented entries as structured lists.

  • Converts owner elevated ? into a boolean.

  • Produces one JSON object per job, separated by newlines (JSONL format).

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse a full BITS job listing into JSONL.

Parameters:

text – Full textual output of bitsadmin /list /verbose or similar.

Returns:

A JSONL string, where each line is a JSON object representing a job.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.dns_cache_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.dns_cache_parser.DnsCacheParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse ipconfig /displaydns output into structured JSONL records.

Each DNS cache block in the input becomes one JSON object with fields extracted from colon-separated lines (e.g. Record Name: example.com).

The parser:

  • Preserves the raw domain name as-is.

  • Normalizes field keys via _normalise_key.

  • Leaves field values untouched (no type coercion).

  • Emits one JSON object per entry, separated by newlines (JSONL).

Example record:

{

“type”: “dns_cache_entry”, “domain”: “github.com”, “record_type”: “1”, “time_to_live”: “120”, “data_length”: “4”, “section”: “Answer”

}

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse DNS cache text into a JSONL string.

Parameters:

text – Full output of ipconfig /displaydns or a similar dump.

Returns:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object describing a single DNS cache entry.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.dns_records_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.dns_records_parser.DnsRecordsParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse DNS zone record listings into structured JSONL output.

This parser is intended for command-line style DNS zone dumps that follow a pattern such as:

* example.com * www A IN 3600 192.168.1.10 mail MX IN 3600 10 mail.example.com

Zones are detected by lines wrapped in *** … ***. Every subsequent non-comment line is parsed as a DNS record belonging to the current zone.

Output format:

  • One JSON object per record.

  • Returned as JSONL (newline-separated JSON objects).

  • Fields are stored as raw strings (no type conversion).

Example output record:

{

“type”: “dns_record”, “zone”: “example.com”, “name”: “www”, “record_type”: “A”, “field1”: “IN”, “field2”: “3600”, “ttl”: “3600”, “data”: “192.168.1.10”

}

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse a DNS zone record listing into JSONL.

Parameters:

text – Full raw output of a DNS zone listing command or file.

Returns:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object representing a single DNS record. Unknown or malformed lines are skipped.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.enumlocs_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.enumlocs_parser.EnumLocsParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse NTFSUtil EnumLocs output into per-volume JSONL records.

Each JSON object (one per line) represents a volume and contains:

  • volume_id, status, filesystem

  • entries: list of sub-lines (each {“key”, “value”, “mounts”: []})

  • flattened arrays for repeated keys:

    • MountedVolume, PhysicalDriveVolume, DiskInterfaceVolume, Snapshot

  • parameters: dict with all values from the global Parameters section

Returns:

JSONL (newline-separated JSON objects), one per volume.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse an EnumLocs text dump into JSONL (one record per volume).

Parameters:

text – Full textual output of NTFSUtil EnumLocs.

Returns:

A JSONL string. Each line is a JSON object describing one volume, including a parameters dict copied from the global Parameters section.

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.handle_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.handle_parser.HandleParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse Sysinternals handle.exe output into structured JSONL records.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse raw handle.exe output into newline-delimited JSON.

Parameters:

text – Full stdout content from Sysinternals handle.exe.

Returns:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object describing a single open handle, enriched with the owning process context.

Example return (one per line):

{“type”: “handle_entry”, “process_name”: “…”, … }

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.listdlls_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.listdlls_parser.ListDllsParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse Sysinternals listdlls output into structured JSONL records.

The parser extracts the list of loaded DLLs (or EXEs) for each process.

Parsing details:

  • Process headers look like: explorer.exe pid: 1234

  • Module table is detected via a header row containing “base”, “size”, and “path/module/file”.

  • Module rows follow the pattern: <base> <size> <full_path>

  • Returns a JSONL string (newline-delimited JSON objects).

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse raw listdlls text output and return JSONL records.

Parameters:

text – Full stdout text from Sysinternals listdlls.exe.

Returns:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object describing a single loaded module (DLL, EXE, etc.), annotated with its owning process.

Example return line:

{“type”:”loaded_module”,”process_name”:”notepad.exe”,”pid”:”1234”,…}

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.netstat_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.netstat_parser.NetstatParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse netstat -ano output (supports French/International variants).

The parser extracts active TCP/UDP connections and emits one JSON object per row containing protocol, local/remote address, state, and PID.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Parse raw netstat -ano text output into JSONL records.

Parameters:

text – Full output of netstat -ano (any language/locale).

Returns:

A single string containing one JSON object per line (JSONL format), each describing a network connection with fields:

{

“type”: “netstat_connection”, “protocol”: “TCP”, “local_address”: “192.168.0.10:49723”, “remote_address”: “8.8.9.9:443”, “state”: “ESTABLISHED”, “pid”: “1216”

}

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.routes_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.routes_parser.RoutesParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse Windows route print output (supports localized output).

The parser extracts:

  • Interface list

  • IPv4 routing table

  • IPv6 routing table

Output is JSONL, one record per interface or route entry.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Convert route print console output into JSONL records.

Parameters:

text – Full output of route print (any locale, typically French or English).

Returns:

Interface entry:
{

“type”: “route_interface”, “index”: “17”, “mac_address”: “94 c6 91 12 a4 3b”, “name”: “Intel(R) Ethernet I219-V”

}

IPv4 route:
{

“type”: “ipv4_route”, “destination”: “0.0.0.0”, “netmask”: “0.0.0.0”, “gateway”: “192.168.1.1”, “interface”: “192.168.1.42”, “metric”: “25”

}

IPv6 route:
{

“type”: “ipv6_route”, “interface_index”: “17”, “metric”: “25”, “destination”: “::/0”, “gateway”: “fe80::1”

}

Return type:

A JSONL string where each line is one of

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.tcpvcon_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.tcpvcon_parser.TcpvconParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse Sysinternals tcpvcon -accepteula -n -c CSV output.

Sysinternals tcpvcon can export active TCP/UDP connections in CSV format. This parser expects that format and converts each row into a JSON object.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Convert tcpvcon CSV output into JSONL records.

Parameters:

text – Raw CSV text as produced by tcpvcon -c (header is optional).

Returns:

{

“type”: “tcpvcon_connection”, “protocol”: “TCP”, “process_name”: “chrome.exe”, “pid”: “1284”, “state”: “ESTABLISHED”, “local_address”: “192.168.1.42:49723”, “remote_address”: “52.109.76.14:443”

}

Return type:

A JSONL string where each line is a JSON object like

src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.wmi_eventconsumer_parser module

class src.osir_lib.osir_lib.modules.windows.live_response.wmi_eventconsumer_parser.WmiEventConsumerParser[source]

Bases: object

Parse WMI EventConsumer text blocks into structured JSONL.

This parser ingests output similar to:

Name : ActiveScriptEventConsumer CreatorSID : S-1-5-21-123… ScriptFileName : C:WindowsTempevil.vbs ScriptingEngine : VBScript KillTimeOut : 0

Blank lines delimit records. Duplicate keys are preserved by converting values into lists. Any unmatched lines are stored in _raw_extra.

parse(text: str) → str[source]

Convert raw WMI EventConsumer dump into JSONL records.

Parameters:

text – Raw text obtained from a command such as Get-WmiObject -Namespace rootSubscription -Class __EventConsumer

Returns:

{

“type”: “wmi_eventconsumer”, “Name”: “ActiveScriptEventConsumer”, “ScriptFileName”: “C:WindowsTempevil.vbs”, “ScriptingEngine”: “VBScript”, “KillTimeOut”: “0”

}

Return type:

A JSONL string where each line is a record like
